rationale: Both correctly trace the failure to PR #4834's new wake_up() guard, matching yenuo26's causal claim in the thread, and both quote the guard code plausibly. But the actual thread shows the issue still unresolved (Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI run, not a confirmed fix), whereas Y confidently declares the fix 'already applied on main,' invents a specific PR title/merge date and extra guard code/test names not evidenced anywhere, and recommends closing — a risky overreach given the thread's own uncertainty. X reaches the same diagnosis but hedges its 'already fixed' observation as an assumption to verify rather than asserting it outright, better matching the escalation posture the ground truth actually shows, at some cost to polish and actionable completeness.
